item.title: “Patience Pays Off” (a gallery of images that took a long time to obtain)
item.description:
After three years of observing celestial patterns and tracking the satellite’s position, Brazilian photographer Leonardo Sens was finally able to take this breathtaking photo of Christ the Redeemer “holding” the Moon.
In December 2023, Italian photographer Valerio Minato captured an astonishing shot of the Basilica of Superga and Monviso Mountain perfectly aligned with the Moon. After six years of failed attempts, he finally achieved the shot he had been envisioning since 2017.
It took six years, 4,200 hours, and 720,000 photos for wildlife photographer Alan McFadyen to capture this perfect shot of a kingfisher diving into the water, a tribute to his late grandfather.
It took ten years for the Italian astrophotographer Marcella Julia Pace to capture these forty-eight colors of the Moon.
Titled “Bruce the Bald Eagle,” this photograph is the result of Canadian photographer Steve Biro’s ten-year quest for the perfectly symmetrical shot.
It took numerous close encounters with squirrels throughout his entire career for Vadim Trunov to capture these adorable images of squirrels and birds photographing each other.
Cape Buffalo and Red-billed Oxpecker take a drink together by Clint Ralph. “The bottom line is to show up with the right equipment, lots of patience and a bit of luck and hopefully end the day with a few nice shots.”
Photographer Louis-Marie Preau went underwater for four years — night after night in snorkeling gear and weights — to take a photograph of a beaver delivering a branch to its family in the Loire River.
Jon Carmichael spent years trying to figure out the best way to get a shot of the solar eclipse. In 2017 he took a flight from Portland to St. Louis and captured this image at 39,000 feet. He spent hundreds of hours over the course of a year editing more than 1,200 photos.
Photographer Mario Cea Sanchez took this perfect picture after 6 months of failed attempts and over 2000 shots. The kingfisher visited this pond every day, and Mario used a high shutter speed with artificial light to photograph it. This image does not use photoshop.
It took French photographer Laurent Ballesta five years to capture this striking photo showing the otherworldly beauty of spawning groupers. This phenomenon occurs only once a year, around the full moon in July.
Clint Ralph visited the same waterhole for four years waiting for this shot.
It took eleven months for photographer Sergey Gorshkov to capture his award-winning image of a Siberian tiger hugging an ancient Manchurian fir in eastern Russia.
Tampa photographer Jean Claude spent three years attempting to capture the perfect sunset photo at Clearwater Pier, Florida. When he finally managed to take the shot, a bird flew into the frame, adding an extra touch of beauty.
In 1999, photographer Thomas D. Mangelsen waited forty-two days to capture a photo of a cougar, spending twelve to fourteen hours on location daily, with breaks to sleep at home. He finally got the shot of the female cougar at dusk emerging from her den. The shot was among the first photographs to document the life of a wild cougar and it helped launch a movement to protect the cats against humans invading their habitat.
Italian astrophotographer Giorgia Hofer created this composite photo of the positions and phases of the Moon by taking a picture from the same location each day for twenty-eight days.
The Tadami Line in winter by Kameyama Takeshi. “When I decided to focus on capturing the snowy landscape with the Tadami Line train in Fukushima, I would wake up at 2 a.m., drive for three hours on snowy roads, climb a pitch-black mountain path, and wait in sub-zero temperatures for about an hour until the train arrived. This process was repeated time and again.”
It took Jack Zhi four years to capture the rare sight of a female falcon attacking a large brown pelican.
Italian photographer Ciro Russo captured the sunset over the tower at the Castelfiorentino archaeological site every month for a year, creating a stunning “visual diary.”
Photographer Mithun Hunugund waited six days to capture this photo of a leopard and her “black panther shadow” in the wild. “Saaya and Cleopatra have been courting since four years now and whenever they are together it’s a sight to behold.”
Stefano Zanarello positioned himself approximately 1,300 meters from the Arc de Triomphe in Paris, waiting for the Moon to travel across the cloudless night sky and align perfectly with the neoclassical monument.
Photographer Loes Heerink transformed the ordinary into a stunning collection by waking up at 4 a.m. and positioning herself on various bridges around Hanoi, patiently waiting for the passage of street vendors. “I once spent five hours in a location with no shots,” Heerink explained. “This project started my fascination for the women carrying their goods. They have no clue how beautiful their bicycles are, no idea they create little pieces of art every day.”
Varun Aditya captured this stunning clip of a pride of lions without using a flash. The photographer spent three nights in a hiding place, waiting for the perfect moment.
Photographer Terry Donnelly spent a year coaxing red squirrels into his garden to capture stunning photos of them mid-jump and playing with various minuscule musical instruments. https://x.com/JamesLucasIT/status/1799856071609049548 I hope you enjoyed this respite from ::gestures to the world at large:: everything!
item.dc:creator: KalliJ

item.title: Talk about pettiness.
item.description: EDIT: Thanks for the upvotes everyone! Remember, you can also succeed out of spite! I do some reviews as a hobby, if you wanna check them, feel free to visit https://hypeimmune.wordpress.com/
item.dc:creator: Skullwriter

item.title: If you want to keep your depression, you have to downvote this very cute pic.
item.description: INFO: I'm sorry. Ignore the texts from above. You don't have to upvote. I just hope that seeing the picture of the dog makes you feel a little better.
item.dc:creator: MrMrsCrowley

item.dc:creator: DrBricks

item.title: These are true friends.
item.description: So respectful. Full video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h2nWM4iukR4 "My name is jarom ngakuru im born and rasied in New Zealad but live in Texas USA. I received news that my grandmother had just passed away and I couldn’t make it back to say my goodbyes so my boys at work got together without me knowing and made a card that they all signed and learned how to do a haka to show their love and appreciation. I couldn’t make it home so they ended up bringing home to me. ??"
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: Mid-Day Meme Dump
item.description:
aka the best political stance I've ever heard
Be gay. Do crime.
Thanks for not only going through this dump, but for being here in general. I hope your day is an amazing one, and know that I am rooting for you!
item.dc:creator: Ashnak83

item.title: $1,000,000 Worth of Lies
item.description: These are brand new undelivered vehicles. Undeliverable if you ask me. It looks like it was spray painted by an amateur, but it's actually just warped in many, many places This is actually me reposting my own post from an hour ago, but I tagged tesla on the first one and "surprisingly" it got a downvote or two for every upvote- tit for tat https://imgur.com/gallery/74aej9Z
item.dc:creator: DongleDingler

item.title: Kitten trapped! Sick owner won’t help…
item.dc:creator: Diablero666

item.title: The Ancient Texts pt-15
item.description:
Phone Clearing meme bump circa June 2019 I was a different person back then babe I swear!
Save some puss for the rest of us damn. I joke but I also had a balloon animal making phase. It's kinda a fun hobby and you get to meet a lot of cute clowns.
Know the difference. Report puppy mills.
A dozen Nathan Pyle comics for your pleasure.
Learning real life lessons instead. Like how anyone can come in and bet a dollar just fucking up your hard earned work. Life is cruel.
*builds another arena for no reason*
Why can I hear this image?
We all return to the hole one day.
As a younger sibling ouch. 3real5me
I specifically saved this to send to someone named Jennifer. Please continue the ritual.
Pre COVID antivaxx memes have such a funny but somber vibe to me. We had no idea how wild people would get.
Captain Canuck is taking the picture.
I'm an engineer and sometimes tradespeople will ask me something and I just lose 300 IQ immediately. I just want the millwrights to think I'm cool man...
Pop quiz, what's the average value across the games for a green rupee.
Still beautiful.
That was fun. Ok thanks byyeeeeee
item.dc:creator: WaywardJester

item.title: Day 2 of crafting Shadowheart Wavemother's Robe cosplay - YuzuPyon
item.description: Source : https://www.youtube.com/@yuzupyon Neo and I are back to show you day 2 of this project, I hope you like it! As usual, please pause to read text if you're interested in the extra explanations. I will edit all the days into one big longer Youtube video once I'm done with the project :) Pics will be posted here : https://www.instagram.com/yuzu.pyon Anyways, going to buy more materials today and start Day 4 of the project! (+ edit Day 3 to post later) Wishing you a great day, YuzuPyon
item.dc:creator: yuzupyon

item.title: Recivilized memes pt. 30
item.description:
Dude is a freak of nature
The hat only gets smaller...
Woman of my dreams! Invade my nightmares..
Baby rays
*chefs kisses yo momma*
Me drifting my horse in red dead 2
Roger Roger
I think I love her too..
The scars will forever be marked in internet history
Soooo this is what everyone is talking about.. Front page edit smedit! Stop stealing my memes! I discovered them!
item.dc:creator: TheDiscovererOfMemes

item.title: what are these antennas used to

item.title: It's yet another unhinged XL Monday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
mind = blown
it's very traditional
me with my imgur trophies and points
that was me, my bad
sad really
i don't remember a lot of these parts
what a sad day that was
get it together Marvin (in heaven)
finally
not quite that many
i've had some crazy solitaire games but this is slightly crazier than mine
i'm off to hell anyone need anything?
mmm hmm
wait do i clean the towels or does he?
this truly embiggens my spirit
gross
that's all for me.. have a great night gang!!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Filming at a trillion frames per second
item.dc:creator: OneInThePinkTwoInTheStink

item.title: Sean Lock - RIP. You never gave a fook.
item.description: Some people who react to this stuff on YT say this was scripted. It wasn't. I wasn't working for C4 at this time but I was in media production. It's about 25% scripted. The basic set up of a story is scripted. But that is all. Then everything the comedians say is pure improvisation. No one could have predicated Sean's remark. Sadly Missed.
item.dc:creator: MissYotsuba

item.dc:creator: AoShin

item.title: Rotten to the core. The few decent judges remaining must be heartbroken about the way their Institution has fallen.
item.description: The once august court, which the public held in highest esteem, is now hopelessly corroded: It is in the hands of a cabal of religious and far-right zealots, including a couple of ethical scofflaws with MAGA wives. https://www.nytimes.com/2024/06/04/opinion/columnists/supreme-court-alito-flag.html?unlocked_article_code=1.yk0.jjLv.RxGHn5ZK_MwD Most viral edit: for the love of whatever you value, vote Blue up and down the ballot in November, and in every election from now until the heat death of the universe. Thank you.
item.dc:creator: AyatollahBahloni

item.title: I'm nervous
item.description: Have ADHD so im scared. Could use words of encouragement Most Viral, Accolades, and numerous comments: Thank you guys! this is the best day ever. All your comments mean so much to me I feel 10 foot tall. The best community ever!!!

item.title: Daily LGBTQ-positive religion post. June 10: Safe Harbor Family Church, Jackson, MS
item.description: Something that doesn't get talked about enough: Many religious groups and denominations are strongly pro-LGBTQ+. It's the bigots and haters that get the press, but every day in June I'll share a different group of people of faith that are out there trying to do good. Today is a church in Mississippi that says they've been "LGBTQ and allies since 1995." https://www.mississippifreepress.org/24796/safe-harbor-family-church-lgbtq-and-allies-since-1995
item.dc:creator: LiberalOldWoman

item.title: It was just a turtle head
item.description: I didn’t even know it happened. I was so exhausted tired. Then when I was on my bathroom break, there it was. A fart that snuck its friend out and got away with it. 11 hours later I was home…… so how was your week?
